'''Ben McNamara''' (born 8 December 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so|sh}} and {{rlp|hk}} for [[Bradford Bulls]] on one-week loan from the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
McNamara is the son of coach [[Steve McNamara]]. 

He played [[rugby union]] and amateur rugby league for the Skirlaugh Bulls.

==Playing career==
===Hull F.C.===
McNamara made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Hull against the [[Castleford Tigers]] and went on to score a try in the 61st minute.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/castleford-vs-hull/teams/54570|title=Starting Lineups - Castleford vs Hull|publisher=Sky Sports |date=1 October 2020}}</ref>

McNamara played nine matches for Hull F.C. in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=5 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 16 Oct 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Leopards]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=16 Oct 2023|title=Ben McNamara's new club confirmed as ex-Hull FC halfback joins Super League team|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ben-mcnamaras-new-club-confirmed-8833889}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 29 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] on one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Bradford Bulls land Leigh Leopards star for Wigan Warriors clash|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-land-leigh-leopards-star-for-wigan-warriors-clash}}</ref>
